data.narrative:
Mistral AI, a Series C-stage AI & Software company founded by Arthur Mensch, Guillaume Lample, and Timothée Lacroix, is evaluated against a comp universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (Palantir). The company has raised $3.0B+ to date and carries a current valuation of €11.7B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis, using the $3.0B total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $5.1B. Peer-Median analysis across 7 same-sector, same-stage comparables—including Figure AI ($39.0B), Skild AI ($14.0B), Neuralink ($9.7B), Luma AI ($4.0B), and ElevenLabs ($3.0B)—yields a median of $4.0B, with a range spanning $1.5B to $39.0B.

Triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $3.4B (low) to $5.9B (high), with a median of $4.5B. The current €11.7B valuation significantly exceeds both implied benchmarks, suggesting either a material uptick in fundamentals post-raise or a premium to peer multiples. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in the peer set ($1.5B–$39.0B), the limited public comp coverage, and the volatility inherent in early-stage AI valuations. Further diligence on revenue traction and capital efficiency would refine this range.
